analysis.reasoning: Core message: The tweet states that every four years major party candidates debate on live TV to sway voters in U.S. presidential elections and invites readers to learn about the history of debates. Evaluation by narrative: US1 (honorable tradition) — The reference to the cyclical, longstanding practice of televised debates and an invitation to explore their history frames debates as a durable democratic tradition, aligning with showcasing a legacy; thus supported. US2 (admirable society) — No mention of societal groups, civil society, companies, or heroes; not supported. US3 (hostile enemies) — No adversaries mentioned; not supported. US4 (force for good) — No claims about supporting human rights or global good; not supported. US5 (great international ally) — No mention of alliances; not supported. US6 (at the forefront) — No claims about leadership or superiority; not supported. Conclusion: Only US1 applies.
